sourceProduct: Mars Express HRSC level-4 DTM, orbit 0037 (H0037_0000_DT4.IMG), MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0, NASA PDS Geosciences Node
credit: ESA/DLR/FU Berlin (G. Neukum), CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO
notes: 20x20 km window centered 18.745N 227.176E framing the ~19 km nested collapse crater on the caldera's northeast rim; 2.07 km rim-to-floor cliff. Elevations are metres above the Mars areoid (Olympus really is ~18-19 km above datum). Upsampled 50 m/px source to ~9.76 m grid cells with cubic-spline interpolation; real information content is 50 m.
